findings.acceptance_test: In the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:193, no standalone narration beat depends on an omitted subject or auxiliary to make sense; the prose may stay brief, but each sentence is grammatically complete on the page.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into clipped, subject-dropped narration ("Poured from.", "Arithmetic she didn't like.", "Human fast.") that reads as a mechanical compression tic rather than controlled prose. This directly violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping and becomes noticeable enough to flatten the chapter's sentence-level authority.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:193 into grammatically complete sentences or fold them into adjacent clauses. Preserve the combat compression and counting rhythm, but achieve it through complete syntax rather than omitted subjects, articles, or auxiliaries.

findings.acceptance_test: A cold read of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:113 yields one consistent footwear identity, and the landslide beat no longer implies that Kem switched back into ceremonial boots off-page.
findings.problem: Kem's boots change identity inside the same march-and-fight sequence. The chapter first establishes trader-bought road boots, then later calls them "expensive ceremonial boots," which creates a local object-state contradiction and breaks physical continuity in the middle of an otherwise concrete action scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile the footwear description at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:113 so the same boots are on Kem's feet in both places. If the later line wants class commentary, attach it to the trader's overcharge or Kem's relationship to the boots without changing the object itself.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised aftermath anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:191, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:213, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:219, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:223, every remaining paragraph must either change immediate pursuit logistics, change Kem and Vel's operating rules, or deepen Kem's unstable exhilaration. No paragraph may remain solely to restate that Kem spent more than Vel or that the Hoarded are seriously pursuing him once those facts are already visible in the scene.
findings.problem: The chapter's meaning already lands when the ruined clearing meets Vel's still face and Kem's grin, but the aftermath keeps certifying the same lesson. The 41-versus-3 contrast and the seriousness of the pursuit are re-explained in multiple passes after the action has already made them legible, which turns a strong arrival into overstatement after arrival.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reshape the aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:191, with special attention to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:213, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:219, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:223. Keep only beats that do new work after the reveal at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:181 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:187: one immediate triage/tactical beat, one concrete new proof of institutional pursuit, and one new operating rule for Kem. Cut or replace later material that merely paraphrases what the clearing already proved.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 4 differentiates its opening from Chapters 2 and 3 by foregrounding a fresh problem within the first page, without another full recap of Kem's Vault conditioning, blisters, and current level total.
findings.evidence: Chapter 2 opens Kem's road plot by stressing that even short walks inside the Vault exhausted him and left him blistered; Chapter 3 reintroduces him through Vel's inspection of the same ruined-road incompetence; then Chapter 4 opens by again comparing the current climb to the tiny distances he walked in the Vault and restating his 286 count and fourteen lost levels: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:294; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:346; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:407; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:433; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:740; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:748.; Chapter 3 already fixes Kem at 286 after wasting fourteen levels and frames his sheltered body through split boots; sunburn; and unscarred hands at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:403; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:405; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:409. Chapter 4 then reopens by restating the same preserved-body premise and exact count at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:740; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:742

evidence.excerpt: 294: Already his legs complained. The longest walk he normally took inside the Vault was the route from his chambers to the Conservatory, perhaps eighty meters, with a pause halfway in the gallery where a bench invited rest. He had already gone farther than that. The burn in his calves had progressed from complaint to argument. A blister was starting on his right heel. The shoes were handmade, commissioned from a Spent Town cobbler who had spent two levels on the fitting, perfect for marble floors and wrong for everything else.

346: Robes designed for sitting. Shoes designed for marble. No food, no water, no pack, no map, no money, assuming money was even physical. The House signet. Two hundred and ninety-five levels of raw ability he did not know how to use without wrecking the nearest door.

407: Four hundred meters farther on, she found him sitting on a fallen log with his boots off and one foot in his lap, studying a blister with scholarly concern. His road boots were soft Vault leather, good for polished floors and not much else. One sole had split. As Vel watched, a pale Glow rose over the raw skin and cost him a level just to inspect his own foot.
433: The answer came after that brief inward stillness people had when they checked the Ledger. "Two hundred and eighty-six."
740: The ridge trail had been climbing since dawn, and Kem's calves were filing complaints he had no language for.

748: His Ledger read 286, a number he felt the way he'd once felt the Vault's walls, always there at the edge of thought, a quiet hum of remaining capacity. Fourteen levels had gone since leaving: the Projection Shield, the Sustenance Charm, the little wastes Vel counted with the precision of someone forced to live by subtraction. The hum was thinner now. Poured from.
403: He was at two hundred and eighty-six, give or take.

405: At twenty she had still been rich enough to miss a spend and keep moving. At thirty-one the Ledger never shut up, and fourteen levels in three days for food, warmth, and whatever else had offended him made her jaw lock.

409: She stayed in the tree line long enough to take him in. He was tall, mid-twenties, with sunburn starting at the nose and ears where three days outdoors had already made an argument with his skin. Cream-and-gray Vault silks were snagged and dirty now, but expensive even ruined. His hands had no calluses and no scars. On the right hand, bright in the afternoon light, sat the Aldraste signet.

742: In the Vault, the longest walk was sixty meters, Conservatory to east dining hall, flat stone, soft-soled shoes, silence. Nobody ran. Nobody climbed. The body was a thing you maintained through inaction, like a vase, and it performed accordingly. Now his body was being asked to carry its own weight uphill through tangled forest for six consecutive hours, and it had opinions.

findings.problem: By the start of Chapter 4, the window has used the same opening rhythm three times: Kem's preserved body meets ordinary road hardship, the text restates how unready he is, and only then does the chapter advance. That repetition dulls the acceleration the new pursuit chapter should generate.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or cut the top-of-chapter recap in Chapter 4 and enter faster through what has changed overnight: the new partnership terms, the altered terrain, or immediate signs of pursuit. Do not re-explain Kem's softness, his 286 count, or his sheltered-body baseline — the reader carries all of this from Chapters 2 and 3. Within the first page, deliver new threat information or a fresh physical consequence of the previous day's walk, then move to the boot treads, silence in the birds, and incoming company.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Every narration sentence must be grammatically complete. Do not use article-stripped noun-phrase fragments, subjectless stubs, or telegraphic fragment strings as a rhythmic device. Isolated level-count callouts ('Twelve.' or 'Zero.') are acceptable; stacked fragment clusters ('Not night. Underground dark.' or 'Too slow. Too loud. Too late.') are not. Achieve compression through short complete sentences, not by dropping subjects and articles.

When a scene beat has landed through concrete image, action, dialogue, or bodily sensation, do not add an abstract summary sentence that restates its meaning in thesis language. If a paragraph's only job is to translate what the reader already understood from the prior image or line of dialogue, cut it or replace it with a new fact, consequence, or pressure. Trust the scene. One beat, one landing.

No character should deliver a multi-sentence thematic diagnosis, balanced mini-speech, or quotable aphorism during a pressured scene. Accusations, confessions, and revelations must arrive through messy, character-true speech: interruptions, self-corrections, false starts, incomplete grammar, practical redirects, and mid-sentence recalibrations. If a line of dialogue can be lifted as a standalone epigram, it is probably too composed for the scene pressure. Rhetorical questions used for emphasis rather than genuine inquiry are not permitted for Vel.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about a world where doing nothing is rational and doing something is self-destruction. Both the comedy and the darkness must be fully committed. Do not soften combat into choreography or spectacle — fights should hurt, cost real things, and leave damage. Do not soften the comedy into gentle satire — the absurdity of the Hoarded and their agents should be genuinely funny, sometimes cruelly so. Do not soften grief into sentimentality or revelation into epiphany. The premise demands a tonal register that holds absurdity and genuine pain in the same hand. When the material is uncomfortable — the physical reality of injury, the moral weight of exploitation, the specific grief of losing someone to a place you cannot reach — lean into specificity rather than retreating to safety. A draft that never risks anything produces a novel that never moves anyone.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in combat, injury, consequence, or emotional extremity is a craft failure. If Vel breaks ribs, the reader should feel the break — the specific wrongness of breathing with cracked bone, the way she holds herself, the pain when she forgets. If Kem is terrified, the terror should be specific and physical: hands, stomach, breathing, the precise quality of his fear. Do not abstract away from embodied experience into summary or generalization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Sable (low) and early-book Kem (initially low, shifting to high). Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, formal Hoarded speech contexts, or bureaucratic register. In casual conversation, unjustified full-form lines should be treated as suspect.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary chapter rhythm aggressively. Combat chapters should be compressed and physical. Discovery chapters should have more room for observation and the surprise of new information. Quiet chapters should breathe — longer scenes, more sensory detail, conversations that circle rather than drive. Intercut chapters (like Ch 11's Kem/Sable split and Ch 17's Sable intercut) should have contrasting textures between sections. No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural shape: vary scene count, dialogue density, pacing, and the ratio of action to interiority. Dark comedy chapters in the back half (Ch 12, 14, 15, 17) must commit fully to comic energy as a primary narrative mode: the absurdity of Hoarded bureaucratic language applied to human exploitation, the grim humor of fighting for your life with single-digit levels, the comedy of precision teamwork performed in emotional silence. These chapters carry genuine stakes alongside their comedy — the tonal fusion is the point, not tonal alternation.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, physical, occasionally wry. Avoids both literary pretension and genre cliche. The world has its own vocabulary (Hoarded, Spent, Ledger, Stillness, the Scour, the Margins) and these terms should be used naturally, not defined in exposition. Metaphors earned from the world's own materials — spending, counting, weight, erosion — rather than imported from outside the setting. The Hoarded institutional register — bureaucratic classifications, procedural euphemism, administrative language applied to human exploitation — is a specific comic instrument in the Margins chapters. When rendering Hoarded documents, policies, or institutional speech, the language should be precise, procedural, and devastating in its indifference.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: comedy arises from situation and character, not from narrative commentary or winking asides to the reader
Ability-use described like video game tooltips: do not produce stat readouts or system notifications in prose
Emotional temperature permanently at maximum: not every scene is a crisis and not every internal moment is an epiphany
Generic fantasy description: landscape, architecture, and weather must be specific to this world's conditions, not recycled from the fantasy-prose commons
Competence porn: do not let characters be consistently impressive — they should fumble, miscalculate, and fail at things that are hard
Thematic underlining: the prose should not highlight its own meanings or signal when something is symbolically significant
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third-person limited, shifting with the POV character. Vel chapters are compressed and physical — the prose should feel like it is counting. Kem chapters are more expansive and curious — the prose has more room for observation and surprise. Sable chapters are structured and institutional, loosening as she changes. The POV break in Ch 13–14 (two consecutive Kem chapters) should make Vel's absence felt: the reader is locked inside Kem's betrayed perspective, and the prose should reflect the claustrophobia of a mind cycling through anger without access to the other person's interiority. The distance should be intimate enough to access characters' internal arithmetic but not so close it becomes stream-of-consciousness.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter texture and scene pressure. Short declarative sentences in combat. Longer, conditional, clause-heavy sentences in reflection. Comma-heavy run-ons in panic or exhilaration. The Ledger's arithmetic should bleed into prose rhythm during ability-use moments — characters counting, weighing, calculating. Quiet chapters should breathe. Action chapters should compress. Two consecutive chapters should not share the same dominant rhythm.
sensory_bias: proprioceptive awareness — the body's internal sense of position, strain, damage, and capacity
physical weight and effort — characters feel gravity, distance, fatigue
the Ledger's feedback — a character's awareness of their remaining levels as a persistent background sensation
environmental specificity — terrain, weather, architecture rendered through what a body encounters
